reasoning:
The question resolves as Yes if any of the listed candidates (Gnangbo Kacou, Laurent Gbagbo, Pascal Affi N'Guessan, Jean-Louis Billon, or Simone Gbagbo) is removed from the electoral list or becomes legally ineligible to run in the 2025 presidential election before July 1, 2025. 

Laurent Gbagbo, one of the listed candidates, was officially excluded from the provisional electoral list for the 2025 presidential election published by the Independent Electoral Commission (CEI) on March 17, 2025. His exclusion stems from a past 20-year conviction related to the BCEAO case; although he previously received a pardon, he was not granted amnesty, directly leaving him stripped of his civic rights and legally ineligible to run for office. Recent reporting confirms that his name remains off the voter rolls and he is disqualified from the 2025 race as of May 2025. Since his disqualification meets the established criteria well before the July 1, 2025 deadline, the condition for a 'Yes' resolution is effectively met.
